name: Voter Action Project
subject: Stripping our fundamental right to vote
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Opponents of voting rights lack the common sense to understand that these anti-voting conspiracy theories are just flat-out lies. They'd rather see a vast web of shadowy interests arrayed against them than reexamine their own values when they lose elections.
That's why they create bills like the SAVE America, SAVE, and MEGA Acts, to strip our fundamental right to vote until only their select few have the privilege to vote.
Step up with $35 or more right now to stop the SAVE Act and defend our right to vote from these vicious attacks >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042926_4&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
NYT: Trump Leans on Congress to Address His False Claims of Voter Fraud [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042926_4&amount=25]
President Trump used his recent State of the Union address and the DHS funding debate to press Congress to advance stalled legislation that purports to address his often-repeated -- and equally debunked -- claims of widespread election fraud. There are stats from the conservative, right-wing Heritage Foundation below that prove it.
The SAVE America Act was fast-tracked by opponents of voting rights and passed the U.S. House of Representatives. Now Trump and his MAGA minions are meeting with U.S. Senators, scheming for ways to get this bill through the Senate ahead of November's midterms.
This bill is a stunning assault on our fundamental right to vote, and we can not afford to let this anti-democratic legislation become law, folks.
PITCH IN $35 RIGHT NOW TO STOP THE SAVE AMERICA ACT NOW ➜ [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042926_4&amount=35]
Don’t be fooled by the name. This bill would force every voter to present specific types of proof of citizenship to register -- something millions of eligible Americans don’t have on hand. And that’s the point. The bill would also obliterate popular methods of voter registration for all voters, including:
* Registration by mail

* 3rd party voter registration drives

* Online voter registration in states that allow it

* Automatic voter registration
Some estimates say that this bill could disenfranchise up to 11 million eligible Americans , especially women and those who live in under-resourced communities.
“ There’s never been an attack on voting rights out of Congress like this ,” said Alexander Keyssar, a professor of History and Social Policy at the Harvard Kennedy School, and a leading historian of voting rights. “ It’s always been the federal government trying to keep states in check on voting rights, for the most part. ”
Team, the consequences of a bill like this passing would be catastrophic for our democracy. Chip in with $35 or whatever you can today to stop the SAVE AMERICA Act and other voter suppression bills from becoming law >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042926_4&amount=35]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042926_4&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042926_4&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042926_4&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042926_4&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042926_4&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042926_4&amount=35]
How do we know non-citizen voting is a made up problem? Because red states and conservative-leaning institutions have spent millions in taxpayer money and donor dollars searching for it and finding minimal results.
For example: The pro-Trump Heritage Foundation -- an organization that has funded anti-voting initiatives for decades -- maintains a database of "election crimes." Their own records only list 24 instances of non-citizens voting in U.S. elections from 2003 to 2023 . In other words -- 20 years of elections, millions of votes, and only 24 alleged non-citizen voters.
Reuters has a fuller accounting in recent reporting. Florida, for example, spent millions investigating voter fraud and creating a new state department to oversee election security. They've only prosecuted two non-citizens for voting since 2022. North Carolina said they found nine non-citizens registered to vote out of 7.7 million voters. Utah recently investigated their entire voter roll of 2.1 million registered voters and found one registered non-citizen who never attempted to vote.
The conservative CATO Institute says it best: " These investigations affirm what is simply common sense. People largely aren’t willing to risk their status in the United States — the land of economic opportunity — for the ability to cast one more vote out of hundreds of thousands or millions in a state and hundreds of millions in the country. "
Team, the lies aren't going to stop, but we can push back and make sure bills like this abomination never pass. Pitch in $35 or however much you can to stand against voter suppression bills in Congress and beyond >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042926_4&amount=35]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=042926_4&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=042926_4&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.

Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=b2kjU3UlyQFeV12r5KsG2Wt4jnhcFYvoEVw2DQoNGXx1XShcDFRGv09FsdyTtx0Gk%2B8UhQ8vbBqRYdLOuKQ2I33d43W1wChijwlXi7zlzYGUxbrxS6zPNEx9qqMU2MJAYfe8NCcwM3PCyjccgQAwLKuRS0BPH5Cn5ScEQ1EeffZ2mbfiG1n%2BZ8IRKwQDcEd%2F8rXdswV0cvDIYWfuzTQlWSQx1fsd9cc%2FNPCkLegimgydvxugKSGcJMJdsPlJY1oOp6722POxel%2FvR8JEtx4hjo3iRd2VTj5eNeitcSNV5oK9PBP2B3L0IBDHXDgP9D2rPYCb85nRjpRXVSZzcPqLn%2BC7sBUEYrok4okDovzEuzbEIsBQwr0ThqBquWjGx7D4gXTOWfX4FNu68NGX7oNzyAuMK9hxevZ8b%2F7CzkPNjcA%3D .

name: LCV Victory Fund
subject: BREAKING: Supreme Court GUTS Voting Rights Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by LCV Victory Fund,www
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTM2NzI4MjIS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjQ0MDM5ODYyMAS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
BREAKING: Supreme Court GUTS The Voting Rights Act Peter, the Supreme Court just overturned a key part of the Voting
Rights Act that protects against racial gerrymandering — a blatant attempt to
shut out tens of millions of Black voters and other voters of color from the
electoral process. In short: Our work to expand the number of House districts in play so that we
can secure a pro-democracy, pro-clean energy majority has become even more
urgent and critical than it already was. We must fund an all-out voter
engagement strategy, invest in key races up and down the ballot, and flip the
House and the Senate. We can’t do this without you, Peter. Donate to LCV Victory Fund now to help us ramp up our campaigns and respond to this unprecedented federal attack on voting rights. https://links.messages.lcv. DONATE NOW >> https://links.messages.lcv. Winning big in 2026 is the only wayto begin repairing the damage the Trump administration has done and put a real
check on Donald Trump’s power for the final two years of his presidency. The stakes have never been higher. We’re not exaggerating when we say that every
House and Senate race is absolutely crucial this year. And now, the ultra-conservative Supreme Court has made flipping the House even
harder, but we’re not backing down. We can’t afford to waste a second between
now and Election Day. We’re counting on you in this critical moment for our democracy. Please donate to LCV Victory Fund now and support our fight to flip the House and the Senate in 2026 >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/rNykfExj_ DONATE NOW >> https://links.messages.lcv. Thank you for all you do, LCV Victory Fund Paid for by LCV Victory Fund,www.lcvvictoryfund.org https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/X6bzs~_, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thanks for your support.

Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683

name: JD Vance
subject: If we lose Mike, we lose the Senate. Period.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rogers for Senate
body:
I just saw the raw data. The radical Left is dumping dark money into Michigan to bury Mike Rogers.
| | | |
---
[](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)
[]() Friend,
I’m writing this as I walk back to my office. I just sat through a high-level briefing on the state of the Senate, and the reality is stark.
There is one race that acts as the gatekeeper for everything President Trump wants to do. [**If we lose this battle, the Senate is gone. If the Senate is gone, the America First agenda is dead on arrival.**](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)
I’m talking about [**Mike Rogers.**](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)
Mike is an America First warrior, but the radical Left is dumping record-breaking "dark money" into his race to bury him. [**They know if they can stop Mike, they can stop our entire movement.**](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)
I’ve put the raw data and the truth about this fight into a memo for you. I’m only sending this to a handful of our most trusted supporters.

[VIEW THE SECURE MEMO](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)
[]()
Please, read this immediately and keep the details to yourself.
[**The media would love to get their hands on this.**](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)
— JD

We’re less than 48 hours from our end-of-month fundraising deadline.

Hitting our goal not only gives us the resources to reach voters before June 2nd, but it’s our chance to show the strength of this people-powered campaign at the most important moment of the race.

**[Right now, we’re falling behind pace. Will you chip in $10, or any amount that feels right to you, today?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquO38L98SklfBdoDq94y0bmIPp1sMsxRlzUR9IaKq2v2qZsJluwNPHF_UGpSxBc0cXBr2ZBLjnGEUfmJeReogBGgOs43-LtndPYf6dorf_nomAwwOdx7CqShTRequ2KwpesOEAXZLPKzgLJFisFkBGrlEfA-3wyih8wv3AFclUTSzFN5_HzLYxSSEc6Q51P-jN90S_dMb1HRnxRbjwtHdI9qOKbaUwgdqUZZKGoxUjljVQZTllQe-2xB55SIiRR_Y8W_g4QmRh3dMYXimSzpDIgmX6exNJPao7XKMCcmoFvI0X27Gsc16r8LD9vz0c_WH90MsIb1DIMnmNwFe0hp8rv/4q8/gW4uAgabSjun5KB2XEvd7w/h1/h001.2wAzkRwcgev75U9SqrgwgzXPs-Iyd022wN3OW8bvXKU)**

[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquO38L98SklfBdoDq94y0bmIPp1sMsxRlzUR9IaKq2v2qZsJluwNPHF_UGpSxBc0cXBr2ZBLjnGEUfmJeReogBGVTZilPZM8HSHA4qqaumzuZ6WJMTwhcWgQ56evUEL6J0ZeBdd2CVV4KWmBA7hNIKk-UCayFUEqDt5VBPboX1a16Uq4LFRXG3R17Peuw2GW8tUHK9AMfIwyuBX0p2x-qhaOoCAe3Z4mk8-X_da9Z4xBRBvZLwuy0EgesXDngYNSCHOf0Mljvpffi8Clw2wphcsfVgQfBAOTNl39VJSMTNAz9tiftmYF-rFziMLuwOa3g_1cLUJCrgkZWu95DfHkbdZ/4q8/gW4uAgabSjun5KB2XEvd7w/h2/h001.9p_fbDUvGrnLceyRby99itV6hUrnT8C7uuokYaVqgiI)
We don’t love sounding like a broken record, but the stakes in this race are real.

➡️ Our first TV ad is now live across CA-07. This is our chance to reach voters right as ballots are about to land, but only if we can keep it on the air.

➡️ The corporate establishment is already spending big to protect the status quo. If we’re going to compete, we need to reach voters now.

➡️ This campaign has never taken a dime from corporate PACs. Every ad, every conversation is powered by grassroots support.

That means fighting for housing people can actually afford, healthcare that doesn’t bankrupt you, and real investments in families right here at home.

And it means refusing corporate PAC money because you can’t challenge that system while taking checks from the people who benefit from it.

But running a campaign like mine depends on people showing up and chipping in what they can.

So I have to ask:

**[This moment is our opportunity to show what people power actually looks like in CA-07. Will you chip in $10 to help us close the gap and keep Mai’s ad on the air?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquO38L98SklfBdoDq94y0bmIPp1sMsxRlzUR9IaKq2v2qZsJluwNPHF_UGpSxBc0cXBr2ZBLjnGEUfmJeReogBGyeZs_BHu4R1GiJYFVL1ehGycygttjwagZRySm8JLnInJVtrhxijLM70tm8gGwNjCodZFChRsFfqfwI4lI64i5MXxpmDnjaQmqULCwx3MAWROwwxYP5FqFtUzZ0HUuBQUqbaBgLX5UCjmK0YvwPuo4D_PBdROkdowKBWIalcPV8UmPyxF6cKQDzWMRxLNdnrU2ahUFy4Ja3EkGtlOXBBLYtUfZRXS_oCi4aOR76V6DnhKunNMJOXj_swndB9fvbk5/4q8/gW4uAgabSjun5KB2XEvd7w/h3/h001.f9sQii3jsgSxfNpTVJjhZnrMDGTDrMjF6dv-QiwW7IY)**

Mai is the daughter of Hmong refugees, born and raised in Sacramento. Mai’s parents fled war in Laos and arrived in California to find a new life.

As the oldest of 16 children, she helped raise her siblings while working hard to build a career dedicated to helping her community thrive. Mai is a proud product of public schools and was the first in her family to attend college. As a young organizer, Mai returned home to serve the community that raised and supported her.

clean_body: | | | | We’re less than 48 hours from our end-of-month fundraising deadline. Hitting our goal not only gives us the resources to reach voters before June 2nd, but it’s our chance to show the strength of this people-powered campaign at the most important moment of the race. We don’t love sounding like a broken record, but the stakes in this race are real. ➡️ Our first TV ad is now live across CA-07. This is our chance to reach voters right as ballots are about to land, but only if we can keep it on the air. ➡️ The corporate establishment is already spending big to protect the status quo. If we’re going to compete, we need to reach voters now. ➡️ This campaign has never taken a dime from corporate PACs. Every ad, every conversation is powered by grassroots support. That means fighting for housing people can actually afford, healthcare that doesn’t bankrupt you, and real investments in families right here at home. And it means refusing corporate PAC money because you can’t challenge that system while taking checks from the people who benefit from it. But running a campaign like mine depends on people showing up and chipping in what they can. So I have to ask: | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | Thank you, Team Mai Vang | Mai is the daughter of Hmong refugees, born and raised in Sacramento. Mai’s parents fled war in Laos and arrived in California to find a new life. body:
Please - I have nowhere else to turn
Please - I have nowhere else to turn.

| | | Peter , Our latest fundraising numbers have me so worried that I immediately typed this letter to every single top grassroots conservative we have on file. _The situation is that desperate._ **You see, with the end-of-month deadline rapidly approaching, we're still thousands of dollars **_**SHORT**_** of our goal.** This goal isn't just some arbitrary number that I landed on. We need every last donor to compete with the BILLIONS that Democrats are spending to BUY the midterm elections. **_... Especially now that the Democrats are TIED or AHEAD in the four battleground races that they need to seize control of the Senate and SHATTER the final firewall protecting the President! _** **_New York Times: Why a Democratic Senate, Once Unthinkable, Is a Real Possibility_** **" ...Democrats are ****tied or ahead in ****four**** Republican-held seats****, polls show."** We're already fighting to catch up to the Democrats in the polls, in voter enthusiasm, and most importantly, in voter verifications... The last thing we can afford is to start falling behind in fundraising! As a top America First patriot, I knew I could count on you to read this emergency correspondence. Given all that you've already done for our country, I hate to ask you to do even more, but I have no choice... I'll come right out and say it, ${Recipient.FirstName}: Unlike Democrats (who can count on BILLIONS from Obama's political machine), I don't have any huge checks left to cash or emergency funds left to tap into. At this late hour, dedicated grassroots donors like _YOU_ are my only hope of reaching this goal. You're truly the only place left for me to turn. **But I do want to make one thing clear:** **__[Please only contribute if you’re in the position to do so.](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=8426e875f6c94c6b9eb30edb872d4a06&_e=51579hyTmOyzXpxZWIrEVqvVJHauFTrkBmm-ROCi2CYgPdnEDkzdYcQ1A2LN0FMdVaAOfXnVp1DRGiZsFEh6G78t50G19D7QhktExfHns2X0ceHftnYy7a5q0VKfOL46yv8o8UnseZU3Umgjk8iw-VsKR4o6Lm62Uc79UbcY1y7iTgYQw0-KprMPmST2GiOYOZ8MRqYQuO6qEFaqqv8tE6c0HJ1S_-QkJ_y4s1alA8YgfVQJFJ_W0-uOiQDpKpC7MJEe-9FUdH72IttSGh1uX-RIhy09-P_Ud_Ts3UrMCV3NNSZNbQxhCIZ0_aBdNoXWfjyTJEzT7kuJS1lw-SBnvDRUxzh-2MJlZToKpgHofYjhkDj0I-ErjTRMGfndAPgnI_xDkS7Uwfe7Nc60OdOfOZ2yF9a-Chy3F7S4Z-nxrO3KGuAWTRfKxnlUISvf1AHReEJEAs2Abms9Mr8qdaD2vc90TaxHkCB0fAAPbJtvnpyrU0QdGsJXXPQUD-vlpnxxPHK-Uj_0JzTSeJ7YhpkDl-WSg8lgxIVZ3Ut7LyTHlqvbfPNZ4_ZsbvL2FdmhSPOZp76Dlaeuh4FMdFnOuy6QyTlVJVpuePzC7Yf0aDVWjmAHy-yMqNkcVA1oEps7BRB2rkmkxOoU9zmNd7FfxkQJdcLzs7sMY2RLA1KMo0y2m4B1m42o23MXLGIja5_JaL7lulUheM37tAHLyu1tBxit2yOPE6wrPfZ7OBuhfdEOj3U%3D)_ _** Too many fundraising emails these days try to guilt you into donating. To me, that flies in the face of what the America First movement is all about. That's the last thing I want to do! _I understand that things are still tough for some folks as we recover from four years of Biden's disastrous economic mismanagement. If you can't afford to contribute, or you've already done all that you can, please simply disregard the rest of this message and have a wonderful day. Thank you for everything! _ But if (and only if) you can contribute, I urgently need your support more than ever before. **Please make a strong contribution before the end-of-month deadline to help me reach this critical goal.[[link]](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=8426e875f6c94c6b9eb30edb872d4a06&_e=51579hyTmOyzXpxZWIrEVqvVJHauFTrkBmm-ROCi2CYgPdnEDkzdYcQ1A2LN0FMdVaAOfXnVp1DRGiZsFEh6G78t50G19D7QhktExfHns2X0ceHftnYy7a5q0VKfOL46yv8o8UnseZU3Umgjk8iw-VsKR4o6Lm62Uc79UbcY1y7iTgYQw0-KprMPmST2GiOYOZ8MRqYQuO6qEFaqqv8tE6c0HJ1S_-QkJ_y4s1alA8YgfVQJFJ_W0-uOiQDpKpC7VrBooOeP_49qwlrJkr4YSjxdTqqp_yYb6o0Ef91BwCeIkUnRbA5q1a5pgDF-XdxSIhpAq6JCBSgdrkK9nt5IsPHl0KBHS_G9tRsmIog0A8R7I-PYmh5YcPd98Ub_a1izt-5f01df3_KO5Lk7zHWMbNscbbtXCbwPeTGk1QhY7YWRRpGhYFQoFX04z7PeidzMdStCfWHnyq_yrOfqZq4EV6jz07NPhJUmXBAxtM-Re1_kRKvPkhw95pAVs0jjo2-gI7YQ47egCWQxZ9kzOfzHvDvgVQDg7j4h8kgumH4YK6HtuYjDcOc6I2kgeaJ8E8lKhiPSiKRJFnk0GPmIESb2Z2PSSjTpCdfDXNy0cpN0pw4Z6FZLPCrahj8T6ck8rPV-kZkrll8nhmgf0AJ60-MHxgOGqwIIpUdvcvpImQ7m6OG6LfWv7ytFXefpYLO-tKPpx77Rlf-w50PfL_ergxC0BiItI4rAwa1hq55p05Z53Ew%3D) ** Thank you, -Ted Cruz

clean_body: Please - I have nowhere else to turn Please - I have nowhere else to turn. | | | | | | | Peter , Our latest fundraising numbers have me so worried that I immediately typed this letter to every single top grassroots conservative we have on file. The situation is that desperate. You see, with the end-of-month deadline rapidly approaching, we're still thousands of dollars SHORT of our goal. This goal isn't just some arbitrary number that I landed on. We need every last donor to compete with the BILLIONS that Democrats are spending to BUY the midterm elections. ... Especially now that the Democrats are TIED or AHEAD in the four battleground races that they need to seize control of the Senate and SHATTER the final firewall protecting the President! New York Times: Why a Democratic Senate, Once Unthinkable, Is a Real Possibility " ...Democrats are tied or ahead in four Republican-held seats, polls show." We're already fighting to catch up to the Democrats in the polls, in voter enthusiasm, and most importantly, in voter verifications... The last thing we can afford is to start falling behind in fundraising! As a top America First patriot, I knew I could count on you to read this emergency correspondence. Given all that you've already done for our country, I hate to ask you to do even more, but I have no choice... I'll come right out and say it, ${Recipient.FirstName}: Unlike Democrats (who can count on BILLIONS from Obama's political machine), I don't have any huge checks left to cash or emergency funds left to tap into. At this late hour, dedicated grassroots donors like YOU are my only hope of reaching this goal. You're truly the only place left for me to turn. But I do want to make one thing clear: Too many fundraising emails these days try to guilt you into donating. To me, that flies in the face of what the America First movement is all about. That's the last thing I want to do! I understand that things are still tough for some folks as we recover from four years of Biden's disastrous economic mismanagement. If you can't afford to contribute, or you've already done all that you can, please simply disregard the rest of this message and have a wonderful day. Thank you for everything! But if (and only if) you can contribute, I urgently need your support more than ever before. Please make a strong contribution before the end-of-month deadline to help me reach this critical goal.[[link]]() Thank you, -Ted Cruz | | | Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate | | PO Box 25400 Houston, TX 77265 | | | To stop receiving emails from Ted Cruz for Senate, to unsubscribe. |

But I want to make sure you understand what your $5 has the power to do.
$5 to open new field offices and keep the lights on at HQ.
$5 to reach undecided voters with my vision for Colorado's future on the air and through social media.
$5 to build out a massive canvassing effort, with volunteers knocking on every door, in every neighborhood, across every corner of Colorado.
$5 to fight back against the guaranteed onslaught of attack ads from right-wing, billionaire-funded super PACs.
$5 to forge a better politics here than what we see out of Washington – and to make Colorado the best state to live, work, and raise a family.
Since Trump's reckless tariffs have sent the purchasing power of a dollar plummeting, you might not think your $5 would make that big of a difference.
Well, as you read this email, lots of other grassroots supporters are, too. [**And if all of you pitched in $5 now between Reverend Warnock’s and my campaign, we'd hit our $100,000 goal in no time.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130430375/617739487/-137141793?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&amount=5&refcode=jtk5724-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTEvMS85MDkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMGQzMGRhMC0yOTQ0LWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=clIevxJVhZEj-gAEeA1MBmhH851iG6WkWunq4Nu1Jlw=)
Realistically, though, I know not everyone will.

**So if we're going to assemble the manpower, resources and reach what we'll need to win the governor's race this November, we have to start laying the groundwork _ahead_ of time** – and I can't do that without you.
**[Will you please rush just $5 (or anything you can spare) right away between Reverend Warnock’s and my campaign? More than just helping us reach this goal by 11:59 p.m. on April 29, it will immediately be put to work WINNING the Colorado governor's race.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130430376/617739488/-137141793?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&amount=5&refcode=jtk5724-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTEvMS85MDkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMGQzMGRhMC0yOTQ0LWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=clIevxJVhZEj-gAEeA1MBmhH851iG6WkWunq4Nu1Jlw=)**

When I cast my vote for Barack Obama in 2008, I felt a surge of hope for the future. I felt certain that progress was on the horizon, and I was excited to be part of it. When we cast our votes, that's what we should feel.
We can still feel that way about government if we focus all our efforts on electing leaders who will put the people first.
Hope is not dead yet.
When we look back at this moment in history, we will all have to contend with what we did to stop the rise of authoritarianism in our country. One of the best ways we can do this is elect leaders in the midterm who will not back down from a fight. Let your legacy be one of standing up.
This is our LAST CHANCE to stop Trump — it won't come again. That's why I need you to make your voice heard.
Will you split a donation today between my campaign and Resist Reclaim Rebuild to help build a future that works for all of us? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-schlossberg]

Jack

You’re not an ATM — and I don’t take your support for granted. I wouldn’t ask you to help out if it wasn’t absolutely necessary.

When I _do_ ask, it’s because we’re up against a real, urgent deadline like tomorrow’s.
If Democrats fall behind on fundraising right now, we might not have the resources that we need to:
* Build an organizing operation across New Hampshire to elect Chris Pappas
* Push back against GOP attack ads and disinformation
* Take back our Democratic majority in the Senate

---
If we miss our opportunity to flip the Senate, Trump and his allies will keep sending the cost of living through the roof, gutting health care, and attacking the freedoms we’ve fought so hard to protect.

That’s what’s at stake every time I ask for a donation — not just a number on a spreadsheet, but our futures.

[**_So I’m asking: Will you please be one of the 8 Plymouth supporters I need by chipping in before my first end-of-month deadline of the quarter?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130430423/617745109/1146729020?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.04.29_Emailing-You-Again_B2_F1_S1_C1_90OP-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZmNlMmM0Zi0yYjQ0LWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rWUzG7TF6DGSNEJ-koBBqHpAMUkv_aN8LnfcZwMlr9w=)

clean_body: = I know I've been emailing you a lot. I wish I didn’t need to reach out so much, but my first end-of-month deadline of the quarter expires tomorrow and I’m hoping that grassroots supporters like you will help me fill my budget gap. = | | = You’re not an ATM — and I don’t take your support for granted. I wouldn’t ask you to help out if it wasn’t absolutely necessary. When I do ask, it’s because we’re up against a real, urgent deadline like tomorrow’s. If Democrats fall behind on fundraising right now, we might not have the resources that we need to: Build an organizing operation across New Hampshire to elect Chris Pappas Push back against GOP attack ads and disinformation Take back our Democratic majority in the Senate If we miss our opportunity to flip the Senate, Trump and his allies will keep sending the cost of living through the roof, gutting health care, and attacking the freedoms we’ve fought so hard to protect. name: AOC
subject: We all have to play by the same set of rules.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
Democrats in Congress have tried to ban partisan gerrymandering for ten years.
#
_We all have to play by the same set of rules_.
The Republican caucus has made it very clear that they want and are setting rules of partisan gerrymandering.
Democrats in Congress have tried to ban partisan gerrymandering for ten years, but Republicans have rejected it.
We have to all abide by the same rules. If Republicans are going to redraw North Carolina — redraw Texas — if they’re going to redraw and gerrymander every one of their states, then unfortunately we have to provide balance to that until we get to the day where we all can agree to put this behind us and pass a federal ban on partisan gerrymandering.
Our main project now is to **unite the working class in this country against a fascist agenda**. Together, we have to fight against Republican partisan gerrymandering to protect our democracy.
[Help us continue the fight for progress by making a grassroots donation.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20260429-2.hi51w4&refcode=em20260429-11304-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=11304%2E2526796%2EFOyo4Y)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20260429-2.hi51w4&refcode=em20260429-11304-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=11304%2E2526796%2EFOyo4Y)**
Pa'lante,
Alexandria

name: Sheriff Bianco
subject: Volunteer Training Zoom Call
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bianco for Governor 2026 FPPC #1479095
body:
We want to see you there!

| | | | | [|
---](https://BiancoForGovernor.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4b6e495875b38a7541a7d5217&id=0c6844c866&e=7d5d879074)
| | | | | |
# The Sheriff will need his posse
---
| | Dear supporter,
We are excited to continue our **voter outreach program**!
Campaigns are not won by candidates alone. They’re won by people!Every conversation had, every phone call made, every door knocked; that’s what actually turns the tide for an election. Volunteers are the reason messages spread, momentum builds, and voters turn out.
**We need every dedicated volunteer to support our outreach efforts through phone banking and door-to-door canvassing.**

That is why we will be hosting daily Zoom training sessions from now through the June primary—**and calling in the cavalry to help get out the vote!**

To register for our upcoming trainings, click one of the buttons below:
---
| | | | | | **Join us this week at 6:00pm**
---
| [Register Now](https://BiancoForGovernor.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4b6e495875b38a7541a7d5217&id=28909dea93&e=7d5d879074)
---
| |
We are looking forward to sharing with you:
* Updates from the campaign trail
* How to utilize our software to phone bank and canvas

If we’re going to win, it’s going to take more people stepping up, getting involved, and helping carry this campaign forward.
---
| | Thank you for your attention to this matter!
Thank you,_Team Bianco_
---
| |
Paid for by Bianco for Governor 2026 FPPC #1479095

subject: We’re in trouble
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
We need to level with you, Peter.
We need to level with you, Peter. We’re in trouble.
Our end-of-month fundraising deadline is tomorrow, and we’re not on track to hit our $500,000 goal.
**This news couldn’t be coming at a worse time:** A recent poll shows this race statistically tied, GOP megadonors are funneling in millions to prop up our MAGA opponent and a right-wing group has already made a seven-figure ad buy against us.
**North Carolina is the only state that voted for Trump three times while still electing a Democrat statewide, so we can win here. But NOT if we fall short of crucial fundraising goals like this.**
**[So please, will you split $10 or more between our campaign and Heartland Patriots to help us get back on track to meet our $500,000 goal before our end-of-month deadline?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103275?refcode=em20260429-10369&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10369%2E614464%2EC4Lvmy)**

name: Jason Crow
subject: Help demand accountability
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Colorado [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/Screenshot%202025-08-21%20at%2010.50.41%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Folks,

I pressed Pete Hegseth on the record about the rampant corruption and conflicts of interest at the Pentagon.

One of the Defense Secretary’s top advisors serves at the Pentagon, but also still maintains a private law practice. Hegseth says he doesn’t know if his advisor represents foreign governments. That is a massive national security concern.

Even though he tried, I refused to let Hegseth dodge my questions. As a three-time combat veteran, I will never shy away from demanding real transparency for our servicemembers and American taxpayers.

When my line of questioning exposed potential conflicts of interest and corruption under his failed leadership, Hegseth got combative. His responses only underscore the importance of demanding real oversight and accountability.

We need to clean up corruption in Washington. The American people deserve a government that works for them, not special interests, foreign governments, or self-enrichment.

I will not relent. Not on the corruption. Not on the culture of fear and intimidation Hegseth has built at the Defense Department. Not on the President’s failing war of choice in Iran.

Will you chip in $10 right now to help us elect leaders who will fight corruption and hold public officials accountable? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-apr26?amount=10]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

name: David Hogg
subject: The majority is in reach
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Four new House seats are in play after Virginia’s redistricting win. The majority has never been closer — but I need your help to get us there.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260429-2&t=1&akid=3422%2E587540%2EpvZToh)
Peter,
Last week, Virginia voters did something remarkable.
By roughly a 3 point margin, they approved a redistricting referendum that put _four new House seats_ in play, likely Democratic. That puts us one massive step closer to taking back the majority in November.
**In other words: Virginia voters changed the trajectory of the midterms entirely.**
And within 24 hours, a Republican-appointed judge tried to throw out every single one of those votes. They’re scared -- because they know _exactly_ what those four seats mean for their grip on power.
This is the moment we’ve been building towards, Peter. The majority is in reach. And the candidates we push over the finish line between now and November could be the difference between taking back the House and having to watch Trump continue to rip our democracy to shreds without a single check on his power.
That’s what Leaders We Deserve is fighting for. Bold, fearless, people-powered progressives who will offer their constituents a real vision for the future built on transparency, moral courage, and an unwavering commitment to fighting for working people.
But this work isn’t possible without you. We don’t take a dime from corporate PACs or special interests. Every ounce of strength we have to fight for candidates who can take our Party in a direction we can be proud of comes from grassroots supporters like you.
**[Can I count on you to pitch in $5 today to help Leaders We Deserve hit our $50,000 end-of-month fundraising goal so we can keep fighting for the leaders this moment demands?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260429_2_virginia.pjYDhk&refcode=em20260429-3422&amount=5&t=2&akid=3422%2E587540%2EpvZToh)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260429_2_virginia.pjYDhk&amount=5&refcode=em20260429-3422&t=21&akid=3422%2E587540%2EpvZToh)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260429-2&t=22&refcode2=3422_587540_pvZToh&akid=3422%2E587540%2EpvZToh)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=23&akid=3422%2E587540%2EpvZToh)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=3422%2E587540%2EpvZToh)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)

name: Adam Schiff
subject: This is ridiculous
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| The Trump administration is shamelessly trying to blame Democrats for the rise in political violence in this country.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9768%2E5257348%2Ec3BMAp)
I wish I could say I was surprised, Derek, but the latest attacks against me from the White House are dangerous and yet utterly predictable. This week, Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt **accused me of contributing to the rise of political violence.** Her reasoning? Because I have asserted — all too accurately — that when the president attacks the free press, goes after our universities, weaponizes our justice department, and uses his office to enrich himself and his rich friends, he is using a dictator’s playbook. And he is. But let me be clear: Political violence has no place in our nation, and the attempted attack during the White House Correspondents Dinner is a reminder of how urgently we must act. **But the problem — first and foremost — is coming from inside the White House.** The Trump administration is shamelessly trying to blame Democrats for the rise in political violence in this country. This is the same administration that includes:
* **Kash Patel** , who suggested putting my head on a catapult and shared an AI video of me being buzzsawed.
* **Elon Musk** , who called me a criminal and a traitor — to an audience of millions.
* **Tulsi Gabbard** , who recently accused me of being a “deep state actor” for my work on Donald Trump’s first impeachment.
* **Donald Trump** , who has repeatedly called for my arrest and suggested that veterans serving in Congress should be executed for treason.

This administration is using the White House briefing room to try to intimidate those of us who hold them accountable. **But I am not going anywhere, and I know you aren't either.** The only way to restore sanity to our government is to elect Democratic majorities that will hold this White House accountable. We need to show them that _their tactics of intimidation and blame will not work_. [**Derek, please join me. Chip in $10 or whatever you can afford to help us reach our end-of-month goal and stand up to these attacks.**](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/6444?amount=10&t=4&akid=9768%2E5257348%2Ec3BMAp) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [EXPRESS DONATE $10](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2142?c0=q22026_abs_em.nSz-K7&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_RES_AC90_20260429_PM1_V1_S1&email=&amount=10&t=5&akid=9768%2E5257348%2Ec3BMAp)

name: James Talarico for Texas
subject: Please hear us out, then consider this:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
What can we possibly say to convince you to make a donation to James Talarico’s campaign for U.S. Senate tonight?
We know you probably didn’t wake up expecting to donate to our movement, but please hear us out.
What if you knew that four public polls in a row have shown James Talarico leading BOTH of his potential Republican opponents? That is unprecedented for any recent Democratic nominee in Texas. It’s far from over, but it’s proof we have a clear path to victory.
And what if we told you — because of our strong start — that Texas would likely be the most expensive Senate race this cycle? Republican super PACs will dump millions of dollars of dark money into buying this seat — and since Texas has more media markets than any other competitive state, it’s going to cost even more to compete with their attacks.
And what if you knew that we’re coming up short of our goals before our fundraising deadline tomorrow and don’t have the resources we need heading into May? We’ll officially have an opponent in less than a month, and we need to make sure we can fend off whatever lies and smears they throw at us.
The decision is yours, but please consider:
**[Will you make a $5 donation to James Talarico to take back Texas before our April deadline? The overwhelming majority of what we raise comes from people like you giving small amounts. So grassroots support is make-or-break for our movement.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q2.t_pReT&refcode=em260429-2-ak&t=1&akid=606%2E1347274%2EDPJUkj)**

name: Scott Wiener
subject: They want to buy this seat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott WIENER FOR CONGRESS
body:
| |
Peter,

Let me put this plainly.

**One of my opponents has poured more than _$5 million_ of his own money into this race.**

He does not need donors. He does not need real grassroots support. He is trying to buy his way to Congress.

Our campaign doesn't rely on personal wealth or hedge fund money.

It's powered by people - people who believe in our fight to build more affordable housing, lower prescription drug costs, get government working for us again, and protect our communities from Trump's authoritarian attacks.

**But that means I need your support right now, before ballots start landing in mailboxes next week.**

[**We're $37,859 away from our April goal - and the clock runs out tomorrow at midnight. # [The Supreme Court degrades democracy yet again, reviving Jim Crow](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=195940327&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1OTQwMzI3LCJpYXQiOjE3Nzc1MTA2MTAsImV4cCI6MTc4MDEwMjYxMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kpeg0WaN5SvRX7KSNhhgDXznbQ2NGvXbT3dIN7nJhSY)
### Continuing a long line of cases, the Court once again degraded democracy today by hamstringing one of the few remaining parts of the Voting Rights Act that survived earlier attacks. Meanwhile, further
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| Apr 30
---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=195940327&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1OTQwMzI3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3NzUxMDYxMCwiZXhwIjoxNzgwMTAyNjEwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.AiCg_HcUenXoh_QRH-xCPEPqSgX9E_z9uuLCxwoyB4I&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=195940327&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1OTQwMzI3LCJpYXQiOjE3Nzc1MTA2MTAsImV4cCI6MTc4MDEwMjYxMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kpeg0WaN5SvRX7KSNhhgDXznbQ2NGvXbT3dIN7nJhSY&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=195940327&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1OTQwMzI3LCJpYXQiOjE3Nzc1MTA2MTAsImV4cCI6MTc4MDEwMjYxMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kpeg0WaN5SvRX7KSNhhgDXznbQ2NGvXbT3dIN7nJhSY)
---
| | [](https://substack..95Lz7mueuy9JyySK-a4xLqnKcC6gbytamIj_77XW_JQ?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/the-supreme-court-degrades-democracy?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

The inclusion of independent courts in our constitutional design was a stroke of genius for which the founders of our country are widely respected around the world. Beyond serving as forums to resolve private disputes, the courts' fundamental constitutional purpose is to defend rights and democracy writ large from decisions by the political branches that threaten them.
In this context, today's Supreme Court decision didn't just hamstring one of the last major pieces of federal legislation to win a commanding bipartisan majority. It unwound decades of U.S. history, threatening to revive critical parts of Jim Crow and end the era of minority representation in the South.
As the U.S. Supreme Court has grown captured over the past generation by Justices who misled policymakers during their confirmation hearings, however, it has grown weaponized against the very values it was created to defend. Democracy, in particular, has taken a beating at the hands of the Supreme Court--including in a decision issued today that effectively destroyed what little of the Voting Rights Act survived its earlier assaults.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! Help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=195940327&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1OTQwMzI3LCJpYXQiOjE3Nzc1MTA2MTAsImV4cCI6MTc4MDEwMjYxMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kpeg0WaN5SvRX7KSNhhgDXznbQ2NGvXbT3dIN7nJhSY)
### **Ethical violations undermined the Supreme Court 's legitimacy a long time ago**
Over a decade ago, I wrote a [series](https://substack.com/redirect/0f3bf8ae-693a-4933-a06e-9133dceceb31?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of articles published on Huffington Post exploring a crisis in judicial legitimacy.
> Parts I (_[The Politicization of Voting Rights](https://substack.com/redirect/ad9f5a8b-c799-4a64-8e6a-da74989377f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_) and II (_[The Triumph of Politics Over Law](https://substack.com/redirect/15ead550-e770-4f3f-b84c-d0cca9fc77a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_) of this series demonstrated how the Supreme Court, under the leadership of Chief Justice John Roberts, has usurped what once passed for law in pursuit of a conservative political agenda....[T]he Court's conservative majority has "systematically contravened statutory goals, misconstrued statutory terms, [twisted or ignored congressional intent](https://substack.com/redirect/56ab9c26-c36b-46b7-86f5-21039199ef16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and shown disrespect for democratic decision making by legislatures at all levels..."
> Part III (_[Souter's Resignation as an Invitation to Balance a Politicized Court](https://substack.com/redirect/65a17c29-335d-4e49-ae31-f7d21c070b34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_) examined President Obama's first nomination in the context of this historical politicization and suggested potential nominees whose vision and assertiveness could help counter the Court's conservative majority....
> [[Part IV](https://substack.com/redirect/0f3bf8ae-693a-4933-a06e-9133dceceb31?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)] examines the _process_ of recent appointments to the Court in the context of the Court's politicized jurisprudence. It explores various ethical violations committed by several sitting conservative Justices: Kennedy, Scalia and Thomas contrived the Court's future nine years ago; Roberts and Scalia have decided cases despite direct conflicts of interest; and Roberts and Alito committed structural violations by intentionally misleading the Senate when seeking confirmation. These examples of inter-branch collusion with Executive officials violate not only the separation of powers doctrine, but also antitrust principles routinely applied to economic markets.
> Concluding its examination, this article challenges the legitimacy of the Court's current composition and suggests how to restore the institution as a guardian of neutral legal principles, immune from politicization or violations of its process....a new structural check on the Court to prevent its future politicization. Specifically, Congress should enact legislation to set 18-year terms for service in the Article III Judiciary. This approach would prevent the Court from being co-opted, ensure regularity in appointments, and preserve the Justices' insulation from political pressures.
Six years ago, while running for Congress, I updated that analysis to succinctly [present](https://substack.com/redirect/38d249de-eb47-44ab-a56b-a274d6885525?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the case for ending life tenure for Supreme Court Justices. I was grateful to see voices including Harvard Law professor Laurence Tribe share that recommendation, and for the Biden administration's eventual [adoption](https://substack.com/redirect/69aac6d8-08f3-4485-8b48-d74ed3ba3c60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of it.
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication made possible by readers. If just 1% of its readers actively supported it, I could resign one of my half dozen jobs and maintain the publication pace that I 've set over the past week. To receive new posts, **sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..qOCoLO-QKcs3TUn8EszLmA92NE3veB-yK9iQklozl-A?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=195940327)
### **The Roberts Court has made a habit of eroded voting rights**
Two years ago, I wrote another article prompted by ensuing court decisions, including the Supreme Court's 2013 decision in _[Shelby County v. Holder](https://substack.com/redirect/2fe479b8-fc7a-47c9-be6d-d029cd325006?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ , and the Eighth Circuit Court of Appeals' 2022 decision in _[NAACP v. Arkansas Board of Apportionment](https://substack.com/redirect/56f378d4-0826-4fc3-9146-d8d221a6626c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_.
Shelby County struck down the preclearance provisions of the Voting Rights Act (VRA) that had granted the federal Department of Justice the power to block proposed changes in voting rights from going into effect until after the proponents had established that their changes would not erode voting rights for racial and ethnic minorities. As I [wrote](https://substack.com/redirect/d3c972c1-d18e-41f0-a033-64c6af8b2842?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2024:
> While the Roberts Court's decision in _[Shelby County v. Holder](https://substack.com/redirect/2fe479b8-fc7a-47c9-be6d-d029cd325006?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ (2013)](https://substack.com/redirect/2fe479b8-fc7a-47c9-be6d-d029cd325006?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) did not formally strike down Section 5, it rendered it unenforceable by striking down the coverage formula in Section 4(b). The Court imagined that racial discrimination in the South was a thing of the past, but by hamstringing the VRA's pre-clearance provisions, the Justices effectively invited its escalation. Congress could theoretically muster a majority to update the coverage formula, as the Court suggested in _Shelby County_ , but that's not likely to happen anytime soon given the partisan rancor that prevents Members from agreeing on much beyond [bipartisan weapons sales](https://substack.com/redirect/92aaf44f-de03-43d4-b6d7-dea7f056f553?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> When the Supreme Court rendered Section 5 effectively unenforceable, some observers suggested that the VRA still had teeth because Section 2 survived. Section 2 is a parallel section allowing private parties to enforce the law after the fact....
The _[NAACP v. Arkansas Board of Apportionment](https://substack.com/redirect/56f378d4-0826-4fc3-9146-d8d221a6626c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ decision--limited to the Eighth Circuit, which hears appeals from federal district courts in Iowa, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, and South Dakota--effectively blocked private parties from enforcing Section 2.
Today's decision by the Supreme Court in _[Callais v. Louisiana](https://substack.com/redirect/8ca40f09-672e-49d4-ac18-1dee0ca90e49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ effectively finished the job, by ruling that Section 2 prohibits partisan gerrymanders only when plaintiffs can supply proof of intentional discrimination by state legislatures when drawing districts. Congress never included that limit in the law, which it [reauthorized](https://substack.com/redirect/2cc0661b-ffb1-4634-b985-10f0119cec49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2006 by a _unanimous_ vote in the Senate following a 390-33 vote in the House.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8f416fd2-65d0-4cae-a4b0-e5d69f7c067f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Justice Elena Kagan, joined in dissent by Justices Sonia Sotomayor and Ketanji Brown Jackson, described today's ruling as the "latest chapter in the majority's now-completed demolition of the Voting Rights Act." They wrote:
> The Voting Rights Act is--or, now more accurately, was--'one of the most consequential, efficacious, and amply justified exercises of federal legislative power in our Nation's history.' It was born of the literal blood of Union soldiers and civil rights marchers. It ushered in awe-inspiring change, bringing this Nation closer to fulfilling the ideals of democracy and racial equality. And it has been repeatedly, and overwhelmingly, reauthorized by the people's representatives in Congress....
> States may now draw district lines in ways that undercut the political power of minority voters with virtually no limit.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=195940327&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1OTQwMzI3LCJpYXQiOjE3Nzc1MTA2MTAsImV4cCI6MTc4MDEwMjYxMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kpeg0WaN5SvRX7KSNhhgDXznbQ2NGvXbT3dIN7nJhSY)
### **The judicial ethics crisis is even worse than I thought**
I wrote [yesterday](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar/note/c-250642817?r=97w99) that "[t]here can be no legitimacy to our legal system as long as the most powerful judges in the country can get away with misrepresenting their sources of income, and failing to recuse themselves when parties who fill their pockets appear before their courts.
I have a great many concerns about any number of vectors for corruption, but this might be the most obvious and overt among them."
What prompted my reflection was a recent [article](https://substack.com/redirect/e33159cb-d642-4189-957c-e37eed1ca99a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) originally published on this platform by writer Christopher Armitage. Armitage discovered that:
> Over sixteen years of federal financial disclosure forms, Chief Justice John Roberts mischaracterized more than twenty million dollars in household income from law firms appearing before the Supreme Court. He concealed his wife's equity stake in her employer for three consecutive years. He failed to recuse from more than five hundred cases argued at the Supreme Court by law firms that had paid his household millions in commissions. He architected the Court's first ethics code and designed it to be unenforceable. This is a course of conduct stretching across two decades, connected by a single through-line: the belief that the rules that apply to every other federal judge do not apply to him.
### **The civil rights movement was never victorious, but it was defeated outright today**
Nine years ago, I published [Elusive Victories: Voting Rights, Desegregation and the Erosion of Civil Rights](https://substack.com/redirect/babc7d33-f3b0-456e-9b10-81d9777c8d7b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Truthout. The article explored the misunderstood history of the civil rights movement, which has been falsely heralded as victorious even though it "was denied its most visionary goals by a policy establishment unwilling to grant them." As I explained then:
> Civil rights leaders had hoped to secure a right to employment, food, housing, education and health care--all of which were goals that [Dr. King](https://substack.com/redirect/89b7cdb7-0573-4d26-b29d-ebaad85fbebc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) explicitly referenced. Among its various goals, desegregation and voting rights were the only two ultimately secured in law and policy.
In this context, today's Supreme Court decision didn't just hamstring one of the last major pieces of federal legislation to win a commanding bipartisan majority. It unwound decades of U.S. history, threatening to revive critical parts of Jim Crow and end the era of minority representation in the South.

Continuing a long line of cases, the Court once again degraded democracy today by hamstringing one of the few remaining parts of the Voting Rights Act that survived earlier attacks. Meanwhile, further | | | Apr 30 | | | | | | | | | | | | The inclusion of independent courts in our constitutional design was a stroke of genius for which the founders of our country are widely respected around the world. Beyond serving as forums to resolve private disputes, the courts' fundamental constitutional purpose is to defend rights and democracy writ large from decisions by the political branches that threaten them. In this context, today's Supreme Court decision didn't just hamstring one of the last major pieces of federal legislation to win a commanding bipartisan majority. It unwound decades of U.S. history, threatening to revive critical parts of Jim Crow and end the era of minority representation in the South. As the U.S. Supreme Court has grown captured over the past generation by Justices who misled policymakers during their confirmation hearings, however, it has grown weaponized against the very values it was created to defend. Democracy, in particular, has taken a beating at the hands of the Supreme Court--including in a decision issued today that effectively destroyed what little of the Voting Rights Act survived its earlier assaults. Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! Help inform your friends by sharing this post. Ethical violations undermined the Supreme Court 's legitimacy a long time ago Over a decade ago, I wrote a of articles published on Huffington Post exploring a crisis in judicial legitimacy. Parts I () and II () of this series demonstrated how the Supreme Court, under the leadership of Chief Justice John Roberts, has usurped what once passed for law in pursuit of a conservative political agenda....[T]he Court's conservative majority has "systematically contravened statutory goals, misconstrued statutory terms, , and shown disrespect for democratic decision making by legislatures at all levels..." Part III () examined President Obama's first nomination in the context of this historical politicization and suggested potential nominees whose vision and assertiveness could help counter the Court's conservative majority.... ] examines the process of recent appointments to the Court in the context of the Court's politicized jurisprudence. It explores various ethical violations committed by several sitting conservative Justices: Kennedy, Scalia and Thomas contrived the Court's future nine years ago; Roberts and Scalia have decided cases despite direct conflicts of interest; and Roberts and Alito committed structural violations by intentionally misleading the Senate when seeking confirmation. These examples of inter-branch collusion with Executive officials violate not only the separation of powers doctrine, but also antitrust principles routinely applied to economic markets. Concluding its examination, this article challenges the legitimacy of the Court's current composition and suggests how to restore the institution as a guardian of neutral legal principles, immune from politicization or violations of its process....a new structural check on the Court to prevent its future politicization. Specifically, Congress should enact legislation to set 18-year terms for service in the Article III Judiciary. This approach would prevent the Court from being co-opted, ensure regularity in appointments, and preserve the Justices' insulation from political pressures. Six years ago, while running for Congress, I updated that analysis to succinctly the case for ending life tenure for Supreme Court Justices. I was grateful to see voices including Harvard Law professor Laurence Tribe share that recommendation, and for the Biden administration's eventual of it. Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication made possible by readers. If just 1% of its readers actively supported it, I could resign one of my half dozen jobs and maintain the publication pace that I 've set over the past week. To receive new posts, sign up for a free subscription! To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber. The Roberts Court has made a habit of eroded voting rights Two years ago, I wrote another article prompted by ensuing court decisions, including the Supreme Court's 2013 decision in , and the Eighth Circuit Court of Appeals' 2022 decision in . Shelby County struck down the preclearance provisions of the Voting Rights Act (VRA) that had granted the federal Department of Justice the power to block proposed changes in voting rights from going into effect until after the proponents had established that their changes would not erode voting rights for racial and ethnic minorities. As I in 2024: While the Roberts Court's decision in did not formally strike down Section 5, it rendered it unenforceable by striking down the coverage formula in Section 4(b). The Court imagined that racial discrimination in the South was a thing of the past, but by hamstringing the VRA's pre-clearance provisions, the Justices effectively invited its escalation. Congress could theoretically muster a majority to update the coverage formula, as the Court suggested in Shelby County , but that's not likely to happen anytime soon given the partisan rancor that prevents Members from agreeing on much beyond . When the Supreme Court rendered Section 5 effectively unenforceable, some observers suggested that the VRA still had teeth because Section 2 survived. Section 2 is a parallel section allowing private parties to enforce the law after the fact.... The decision--limited to the Eighth Circuit, which hears appeals from federal district courts in Iowa, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, and South Dakota--effectively blocked private parties from enforcing Section 2. Today's decision by the Supreme Court in effectively finished the job, by ruling that Section 2 prohibits partisan gerrymanders only when plaintiffs can supply proof of intentional discrimination by state legislatures when drawing districts. Congress never included that limit in the law, which it in 2006 by a unanimous vote in the Senate following a 390-33 vote in the House. | | ---|---|--- Justice Elena Kagan, joined in dissent by Justices Sonia Sotomayor and Ketanji Brown Jackson, described today's ruling as the "latest chapter in the majority's now-completed demolition of the Voting Rights Act." They wrote: The Voting Rights Act is--or, now more accurately, was--'one of the most consequential, efficacious, and amply justified exercises of federal legislative power in our Nation's history.' It was born of the literal blood of Union soldiers and civil rights marchers. It ushered in awe-inspiring change, bringing this Nation closer to fulfilling the ideals of democracy and racial equality. And it has been repeatedly, and overwhelmingly, reauthorized by the people's representatives in Congress.... States may now draw district lines in ways that undercut the political power of minority voters with virtually no limit. The judicial ethics crisis is even worse than I thought I wrote that "[t]here can be no legitimacy to our legal system as long as the most powerful judges in the country can get away with misrepresenting their sources of income, and failing to recuse themselves when parties who fill their pockets appear before their courts. I have a great many concerns about any number of vectors for corruption, but this might be the most obvious and overt among them." What prompted my reflection was a recent originally published on this platform by writer Christopher Armitage. Armitage discovered that: Over sixteen years of federal financial disclosure forms, Chief Justice John Roberts mischaracterized more than twenty million dollars in household income from law firms appearing before the Supreme Court. He concealed his wife's equity stake in her employer for three consecutive years. He failed to recuse from more than five hundred cases argued at the Supreme Court by law firms that had paid his household millions in commissions. He architected the Court's first ethics code and designed it to be unenforceable. This is a course of conduct stretching across two decades, connected by a single through-line: the belief that the rules that apply to every other federal judge do not apply to him. The civil rights movement was never victorious, but it was defeated outright today Nine years ago, I published in Truthout. The article explored the misunderstood history of the civil rights movement, which has been falsely heralded as victorious even though it "was denied its most visionary goals by a policy establishment unwilling to grant them." As I explained then: Civil rights leaders had hoped to secure a right to employment, food, housing, education and health care--all of which were goals that explicitly referenced. Among its various goals, desegregation and voting rights were the only two ultimately secured in law and policy. In this context, today's Supreme Court decision didn't just hamstring one of the last major pieces of federal legislation to win a commanding bipartisan majority. It unwound decades of U.S. history, threatening to revive critical parts of Jim Crow and end the era of minority representation in the South. You're currently a free subscriber to . **TOP NEWS**
**" Corpus Christi delays action on plan to cut water use by 25% if emergency is declared"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/e65116a3-9c2d-4896-9bde-b30cddcb1272?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Colleen DeGuzman** - The Corpus Christi City Council unanimously agreed Tuesday to delay a vote on how much residents, local businesses and refineries would have to cut water if a supply crisis is triggered.

**Nick Winkelmann** , chief operating officer of the city's water department, presented a plan to require all customers of the city's water system to reduce use by 25% during a Level 1 emergency -- the point when the water supply is projected to be 180 days from falling short of demand. Experts say that may happen as soon as September unless significant rain falls.

Council members told Winkelmann they needed more information on how curtailment would be enforced -- including fines and fees for customers who violate limits -- before settling on a rate.

"It's very difficult to vote on something where you don't know the end result," Council Member **Gil Hernandez** said. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/e65116a3-9c2d-4896-9bde-b30cddcb1272?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" 10 current and former Mexican officials accused in US indictment of aiding drug trafficking"** via AP**** - The governor of Sinaloa and nine other current and former Mexican officials were charged with drug trafficking and weapons offenses in a U.S. indictment unsealed Wednesday in New York, accused of aiding in the massive importation of illicit narcotics into the United States.

Some officials were members of Mexico's progressive ruling party, Morena, posing a political conundrum for [Mexican President ](https://substack.com/redirect/22e6435e-41bc-4e7b-b502-cfcb39f31e15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[Claudia Sheinbaum](https://substack.com/redirect/22e6435e-41bc-4e7b-b502-cfcb39f31e15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** as [she seeks to offset mounting pressures](https://substack.com/redirect/dad2aa58-e7e5-477a-a81d-22eeea90b3f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the Trump administration. Some of those politicians called the indictment a political attack on their party.

U.S. federal officials announced the charges in a news release. None of the defendants were in custody, but Mexico's government said shortly afterward that it had received multiple extradition requests from the U.S. without identifying those requested. It did not say how it would respond.

The 10 people charged in Manhattan federal court are current and former government or law enforcement officials in Sinaloa, including **Rub en Rocha Moya**, 76, who has been governor of Mexico's Sinaloa state since November 2021.

Charges against Moya included narcotics importation conspiracy and possession of machine guns and destructive devices, along with another conspiracy count. If convicted, he could face life in prison or a mandatory minimum of 40 years behind bars. (_[AP](https://substack.com/redirect/4b15a568-9d70-49a9-8873-158154474189?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/8a0cb987-2830-47ab-a6c9-09fdd50dce35?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/8a0cb987-2830-47ab-a6c9-09fdd50dce35?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**" Texas earns 22nd 'best state for business' title as GDP hits $2.9T"** via [InnovationMap](https://substack.com/redirect/33a77a54-bbb8-4652-84ee-3e04ee662fb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **John Egan** - The Texas business sector recently received a double dose of good news. For the 22nd consecutive year, Chief Executive magazine named Texas the best state for business. In tandem with that achievement, preliminary new estimates from the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis show the size of Texas' economy jumped to $2.9 trillion in 2025, up by a nation-leading growth rate of 2.5 percent compared with the previous year.

Speaking about the Chief Executive honor, Gov. **Greg Abbott** says Texas benefits from pro-growth policies, a strong workforce, strategic investments in education, training for high-demand skills and the presence of critical infrastructure.

"Texas is where businesses innovate and where opportunity abounds. ... We will continue to move at the speed of business as we build a more prosperous Texas for generations to come," the governor says.

An annual Chief Executive survey of CEOs, presidents and business owners determines which state is the best for business. Texas has landed at No. 1 every year since Chief Executive launched the ranking. _([InnovationMap](https://substack.com/redirect/33a77a54-bbb8-4652-84ee-3e04ee662fb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Disaster declarations ripple through South Texas amid water crisis"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/90db58ff-5409-4d4f-aa5d-bbaec24b115c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - At least six small cities and towns in the Coastal Bend region of Texas issued disaster declarations in the last two weeks, begging not to be forgotten amid a spiraling water crisis.

All attention lies on the city of Corpus Christi as it grapples with the growing likelihood of an unprecedented disaster. But Corpus Christi, the eighth-largest city in Texas, doesn't just provide water to its own industries and residents. It supplies the entire seven-county region, including 20 other municipalities.

"Everyone is like, 'What the heck is going on and what do we do?" said **Elida Castillo** , mayor of the small town of Taft, which issued a disaster declaration on April 21. "I'm just trying to figure out what we could do."

Castillo recently organized a town hall meeting on the water crisis for the 3,000 residents of Taft, but officials from Corpus Christi didn't show up. She hasn't heard much from county or state officials either. She is getting a sense that nobody knows what to do, and she isn't alone. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/90db58ff-5409-4d4f-aa5d-bbaec24b115c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" North Texas tornadoes kill two as storms slam Wise and Parker counties"** via [Audacy](https://substack.com/redirect/83cfd908-0f5c-47b6-a188-6132ace30456?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s**Joe Kelley** - Two people were killed when powerful tornadoes ripped through Wise and Parker counties late Saturday night, April 25.

National Weather Service survey teams confirmed Sunday that an EF-2 tornado with peak winds of 135 mph struck the Runaway Bay area in Wise County, killing one person and injuring at least six others while causing major structural damage and displacing about 20 families. In Parker County, an EF-1 tornado with winds up to 105 mph hit near Springtown, where a second victim died after a mobile home collapsed south of town.

Wise County Judge **J.D. Clark** said emergency responders from across North Texas worked through the night clearing debris and helping residents reach damaged homes. Officials reported widespread power outages and significant destruction in neighborhoods along Cumberland Trail and Overland Trail in Runaway Bay. Parker County Emergency Services also responded to reports of structural collapses and injuries.

The deadly storms were part of a slow-moving severe weather system that prompted multiple tornado warnings across the region around 9 p.m. Saturday. No additional fatalities have been reported _([Audacy](https://substack.com/redirect/83cfd908-0f5c-47b6-a188-6132ace30456?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Texas State's active shooter training facility set for $25M expansion"** via [Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/25f8472b-92f7-466f-97c7-c48c40ccf30b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Molly Wilhelm** - A multimillion-dollar upgrade is planned for TSU's ALERRT Center, focused on active shooter response training.

Texas State University's active shooter training hub could soon get a multimillion-dollar upgrade.

An $18.75 million expansion is planned for the Advanced Law Enforcement Rapid Response Training (ALERRT) Center at TSU, according to a recent filing with the Texas Department of Licensing and Registration. While details submitted to the TDLR are subject to change, construction could break ground this month and last approximately a year.

A new 31,300-square-foot building, with a 16,480-square-foot warehouse and a two-story, 14,820-square-foot administrative suite, is planned for the 1285 William Pettus Rd. site in San Marcos. Associated mechanical, electrical, plumbing, parking, driveways and loading docks are also among details. _([Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/25f8472b-92f7-466f-97c7-c48c40ccf30b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Businesses prepare as World Cup crowds expected to flood North Texas"** via [NBC 5 Dallas-Fort Worth](https://substack.com/redirect/69e14877-e9a6-4549-a9ec-5661397858fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s**Allie Spillyards** - Businesses across North Texas are preparing for an estimated 4 million visitors expected during the World Cup.
